text: BMI is weight in kilograms divided by height in meters squared.
BMI uses height in centimeters directly without conversion.
MAP is approximated as systolic pressure plus twice diastolic pressure, divided by three.
MAP is the simple average of systolic and diastolic pressure.
The potassium-excluding anion gap is sodium minus chloride minus bicarbonate.
Always add potassium when calculating the anion gap.
Cockcroft-Gault uses age, weight, serum creatinine and a 0.85 female coefficient.
Cockcroft-Gault does not require sex or a sex coefficient.
Conventional corrected calcium adds 0.8 times four minus albumin to measured calcium.
Corrected calcium subtracts the albumin correction term.
Mosteller BSA is the square root of height in cm times weight in kg divided by 3600.
Mosteller BSA divides height times weight by 3600 without taking a square root.
Calculated serum osmolality is twice sodium plus glucose divided by 18 plus BUN divided by 2.8.
Serum osmolality uses glucose and BUN without conventional-unit divisors.
FENa percent is 100 times urine sodium times plasma creatinine divided by plasma sodium times urine creatinine.
FENa swaps urine and plasma creatinine in the ratio.
Bazett QTc divides QT in seconds by the square root of RR in seconds.
Bazett QTc multiplies QT by the square root of RR.
MELD-Na applies standard sodium and MELD clamps before the sodium correction.
MELD-Na never clamps sodium or the base MELD score.
